trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift (set code DFT, released 2025-02-14) is the 103rd Magic expansion, staging the Ghirapur Grand Prix across three planes — Avishkar (renamed Kaladesh), Muraganda, and the post-apocalyptic plane Gastal — with ten competing teams racing for the Aetherspark. [Scryfall API; Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1]

The Endriders are Aetherdrift's black-red faction, hailing from Gastal — a dying apocalyptic plane of gas, draconic warlords, and dry lightning. Their leader Far Fortune seeks the Aetherspark to open water to her drought-stricken homeworld. The faction is explicitly coded as apocalypse-punk road warriors. [Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1; MTG Wiki: Endriders via WebSearch snippet]

Flavor text: "Endrider magic was developed on a world that was constantly on the move — the higher the velocity, the stronger the magic." [Scryfall] This line directly grounds the Start your engines! / speed mechanic in Endrider faction identity: velocity as power source.

At Max Speed (speed 4), Endrider Catalyzer taps for {R}{R} — functioning as a mana doubler for red spells once the engine is running. As a {1}{R} common it is pauper-legal and standard-legal (as of release). EDHREC rank 12,678 places it in the low-interest tier for Commander. [Scryfall]

Illustrated by Karl Kopinski, who has painted 536+ Magic cards across 15+ years of the game — making him one of the most prolific MTG artists in the game's history. [planeswalkersmtg.com; karlkopinski.com via WebSearch]
